s2.q: What is the primary purpose of the Non-stress Test (NST)?
options: To assess fetal heart rate response to contractions
To evaluate fetal heart rate acceleration in response to fetal movement as a sign of fetal health
To determine placental blood flow via Doppler
s2.explain: The NST evaluates fetal heart rate accelerations coinciding with fetal movements. The presence of ≥2 accelerations (≥15 bpm, ≥15 seconds) within 20 minutes indicates a reactive (normal) test, reflecting an intact fetal CNS-autonomic pathway.

s2.q: A reactive (normal) NST requires the presence of:
options: At least one acceleration of 10 bpm above baseline lasting 10 seconds
Two or more accelerations that peak at ≥15 bpm above baseline, each lasting ≥15 seconds, within 20 minutes
Baseline variability of less than 5 bpm
At least three contractions in a 10-minute window
s2.explain: A reactive NST is defined by ≥2 accelerations of ≥15 bpm above baseline, each lasting ≥15 seconds, all occurring within 20 minutes of starting the test. This pattern reliably predicts a healthy, non-acidotic fetus.

s2.q: After what gestational age is the NST typically performed?
s2.explain: The NST is typically performed after 32 weeks' gestation. Fetal movement counting begins at 30 weeks, and if abnormal, the NST is the next step. Before 32 weeks, the fetal autonomic nervous system may not yet produce consistent accelerations.

s2.q: More frequent NST testing is recommended for which of the following conditions?
options: Post-term pregnancy, type 1 diabetes, fetal growth restriction, or maternal hypertension
Women with a history of prior cesarean section
s2.explain: While the standard NST interval is 7 days, more frequent testing is indicated for high-risk conditions: post-term pregnancy, type 1 diabetes mellitus, fetal growth restriction, and maternal hypertension, as these increase the risk of placental insufficiency.

s2.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s2.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.
